title: Web Triage → Identify the Server, Route to the Attack
body: First contact with any HTTP service. Fingerprint it, check its verbs, glance at it - then ROUTE. This node's job is identify-and-route, not run-every-tool. If nothing's obvious, send yourself to web_fuzz, then come back and re-triage what you find.
cmd:
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# WEB TRIAGE - what IS this server? (fingerprint BEFORE you fuzz)
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# -- STEP 1: Fingerprint the stack ---------------------------
whatweb http://$ip
curl -s -I http://$ip                       # server header, redirects
curl -s http://$ip/robots.txt               # free path disclosure
# Note the SERVER: IIS -> .aspx world.  Apache/nginx+PHP -> .php world.

# -- STEP 2: Check HTTP verbs (the step everyone skips) ------
curl -s -i -X OPTIONS http://$ip/ | grep -i allow
# PUT / DELETE / MOVE / MKCOL / PROPFIND in Allow = WebDAV is ON.
#   On IIS that's a near-direct upload->RCE. Take the WebDAV branch below.
nmap -p80,443 --script http-methods,http-webdav-scan $ip

# -- STEP 3: Eyeball it --------------------------------------
# Open it in a browser / curl the body. Look for: CMS names, login forms,
# ?params= in URLs, version strings, upload features, default pages.

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FINDING → WHERE TO GO  (this map IS the node)
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#  IIS + OPTIONS shows PUT/MOVE ....... webdav   (upload -> RCE)
#  IIS, no WebDAV .................... iis_aspx  (aspx shell path)
#  WordPress ......................... wordpress
#  XAMPP / Apache+PHP+MariaDB ........ xampp_default
#  Grav CMS .......................... grav_cms
#  Jenkins (8080/8443) ............... jenkins
#  ?id= / ?file= / ?page= in URL ..... param_found (LFI/SQLi/SSTI)
#  Login form ........................ login_page
#  File upload feature ............... file_upload
#  Backup/config/.git/source leak .... creds_found
#  Nothing identifiable yet .......... web_fuzz  (then COME BACK here)
warn: Fingerprint and check verbs (OPTIONS) BEFORE fuzzing - one OPTIONS request reveals WebDAV (upload->RCE) that directory fuzzing never finds. Match shells to the stack: IIS=.aspx, Apache/PHP=.php. Enumeration is a LOOP - when web_fuzz finds /admin, come back here and re-triage /admin before grinding.

choices.label: Nothing obvious yet → fuzz for content
